title: andira inermis
intro: Andira inermis, also known as the Brazil nut tree or Guapuru, is a deciduous tree native to the tropical forests of South America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Andira inermis include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the tree's bark and seeds are often used to make teas, infusions, and tonics that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
